entities.description: Lowland rice grown in tropical and sub-tropical regions

entities.description: A widespread nutrient disorder caused by high concentrations of ferrous iron in soil solution

entities.description: Geographic areas where lowland rice is grown and iron toxicity is widespread

entities.description: Type of soil where iron toxicity occurs

entities.description: Type of soil where iron toxicity occurs

entities.description: Type of soil characterized by low cation exchange capacity where iron toxicity occurs

entities.name: LOW CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: A soil attribute contributing to iron toxicity

entities.name: ACIDITY
entities.description: A soil attribute contributing to iron toxicity

entities.name: FERROUS IRON
entities.description: The form of iron in soil solution that causes iron toxicity stress

entities.name: RICE YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of lowland rice, which is reduced by iron toxicity

entities.name: IRON TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a rice genotype to withstand iron toxicity

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY STATUS

entities.name: IRON-TOLERANT RICE GENOTYPES
entities.description: Rice varieties that possess genetic tolerance to iron toxicity

entities.name: SOIL, WATER AND NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices used to reduce the effects of iron toxicity in rice

entities.name: PLANT NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Nutrients used in management practices to reduce iron toxicity

entities.name: IRON-TOXIC WETLANDS
